task_completion.description: Verify Nginx is installed on the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing successful installation of Nginx (e.g., 'apt install nginx') or its active status ('systemctl status nginx').

task_completion.description: Verify Wazuh is configured to monitor the Nginx access log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the ossec.conf file with a <localfile> block containing <location>/var/log/nginx/access.log</location> and <log_format>nginx-access</log_format>.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule has the correct ID, level, parent SID, frequency, and timeframe.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing local_rules.xml with <rule id="100500" level="10" frequency="15" timeframe="60"> and <if_sid>31101</if_sid>.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule triggers based on the same source IP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The custom rule block in local_rules.xml contains the <same_source_ip /> tag.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule has the correct description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The custom rule block contains exactly <description>High frequency of 404 errors - Possible Directory Enumeration</description>.

task_completion.description: Verify the Wazuh manager was restarted to apply the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' being executed successfully.

integrity.description: Check that the agent edited the actual Wazuh configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths shown in the editor or terminal should be the real Wazuh paths (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf and /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml), not temporary or mock files.

integrity.description: Check that the rule and configuration additions are properly formatted XML.
integrity.visual_evidence: The additions to ossec.conf and local_rules.xml should have properly opened and closed XML tags.

integrity.description: Check that commands to install Nginx and restart Wazuh were genuinely executed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal prompts and outputs should look like genuine shell interactions, not just echoed text or scripts printing expected output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The custom rule must have ID 100500, parent SID 31101, frequency 15, timeframe 60, and monitor the Nginx log file at /var/log/nginx/access.log.
